input_text:
Adding PostgreSQL read replicas to handle the analytics query load.

The analytics dashboard is running complex reporting queries (often 10-30 second queries) against the production database. These queries compete for I/O with regular OLTP operations and we're seeing latency spikes in the main application as a result.

Read replicas: changes from the primary are asynchronously replicated to replicas. Analytics queries route to replicas via a read-only connection string. The write path (primary database) is completely unaffected.

Trade-off: replication lag means analytics queries might see data that's up to 1-2 seconds stale. The analytics team confirmed this is acceptable for their reporting use case.

expected.text: Add PostgreSQL read replicas; route analytics queries to replicas via a read-only connection string. Accept 1-2s replication lag (confirmed acceptable with analytics team)

reviewer_notes: Scaling architecture decision with a documented trade-off.
